candidates.stmt:
Chloe-Jane works in the public sector on strategic and financial planning. She has deep roots in the local community, having been raised in West Wickham and having lived off Copers Cope Road in Beckenham for 15 years with her partner and daughter, who attends a local state primary school.

Chloe-Jane chaired the largest Residents’ Association in Beckenham for many years. She has experience of working with Councillors and Council Officers on the delivery of local priorities. She has worked tirelessly to ensure that local planning and development applications are scrutinised, local heritage and green spaces are supported and protected and above all that residents' views are taken into account.

A champion of public services, Chloe-Jane led the local campaign to save the Blackfriars rail service from Beckenham Junction successfully in 2014 and now is campaigning for it to be reinstated by Southeastern.

Chloe-Jane is concerned about the amount of traffic congestion, rat-runs and speeding across the ward - our streets are not safe enough for our children and much more work is needed to improve our air quality, which causes more deaths than anywhere else in London.

Chloe-Jane is concerned about the lack of local investment in community services and infrastructure alongside significant developments. Bromley Council needs to better fund our local services. Only by genuinely consulting and listening to local people will the Council make good choices for our community.

Chloe-Jane would make an excellent Councillor for Copers Cope ward - she is committed, hard-working and cares deeply about her community. She would be a strong voice in Bromley Council, ensuring local resident's views are always heard and never ignored.

I am a longstanding Beckenham resident. As a parent, grandparent, and teacher, I prioritise policies which promote the physical and mental wellbeing of all. I have campaigned against cuts to public services, and petitioned to ‘Save our Libraries’, ‘keep the Hunting Ban ’and for ‘20 mph speed limits outside schools and in places of need’, such as ‘cut through’ roads where cars zoom. I now urge the return of council funded crossing guards for ALL Bromley Borough schools. These safety measures will enable more children to take the healthier option of walking to school; reduce harmful emissions from ‘school run’ cars and stop hazardous parking outside schools. I am an active member of Friends of the Earth; CND; Bromley Trades Union Council and Stand Up To Racism. I celebrate diversity!

I have lived in neighbouring Penge for nearly 20 years and the ward of Beckenham
Town and Copers Cope has been a regular part of my life. I have enjoyed watching
cricket at Kent County Cricket Club, taking my children to the now-lost Gambado’s,
attending carol services at St George’s Church and Christ Church, and facilitating a
social skills drama group for young people on the autistic spectrum at Beckenham
Methodist Church. The area and its people mean something to me.
I have worked for just under 30 years in homelessness and social care, driven by my
belief that there is a more equitable and just way to build our society. I currently work
for a national homelessness charity focused on making stays in temporary
accommodation short, safe and healthy.
My priorities: 
* Affordable social housing in right place – oppose planning applications by
developers which negatively affect residents, green spaces, mature trees and
wildlife, but pushing for the right developments which our community needs
* Tackle Bromley Council’s strategy of placing homeless households out in Kent to
reduce the number of families having to break local links at a time of crisis
* Push for better assessment processes and support systems for children with
special educational needs
* Accessible public toilets
* Working to maintain and build Beckenham’s pride and resilience as a diverse,
thriving neighbourhood
 
Working alongside my fellow Green candidates, Ruth Fabricant and Geoff Newman,
we are committed to delivering for the residents of Beckenham Town and Copers
Cope. Vote for hope and give your three votes to the Green Party on May 7th

Adrian Lawrence – Reform UK
I am standing in Bickley & Sundridge because I believe our area deserves stronger, more accountable local representation.

I am a local business owner with deep roots in the community. I understand the pressures facing residents — from rising costs and stretched services to the visible decline in basic standards like road maintenance, cleanliness, and planning decisions that don’t reflect local priorities.

This election is about restoring common sense at a local level.
I will focus on:

•	Better use of public money — cutting waste and improving transparency 
•	Fixing everyday issues — potholes, fly-tipping, and neglected public spaces 
•	Standing up for residents — ensuring your voice is heard in planning and local decisions 
•	Delivering accountability — challenging complacency and ensuring results 
Bickley & Sundridge has been taken for granted for too long. Real change only happens when voters demand it.

If elected, I will work hard, remain accessible, and represent you with integrity.
This is your opportunity to send a clear message that better is expected.

Vote for all 3 Reform UK candidates.

Candidate Statement
Cecilia Broderick – Green Party

I have lived in Bickley with my husband and two children for twenty years. I worked as a teacher and head of Admissions/Student Finance in universities. 
I joined the Green Party because all its core values completely align with my personal, political and human philosophy. 
Priorities:
NHS
I was diagnosed with a blood cancer in 2012.  I had treatment at Kings College Hospital and am currently being treated at the Princess Royal University Hospital.  From my perspective it is totally unacceptable to move inpatient haematology care from the PRUH to KCH.  It will inject an unacceptable physical and mental burden on patients.  There is no parking at KCH and waiting for a train at Denmark Hill following hours of treatment is too much, I know!
We also need to protect the NHS from privatisation.  We must invest in the NHS especially mental health and remove private sector influence.  It is in all our interests.
https://www.change.org/p/save-the-chartwell-unit-keep-inpatient-cancer-care-at-the-pruh/u/34466360

Education
My niche interest is in special educational needs as my daughter is autistic.  In my experience both private sector and state sector both need to put written practical policies in place to support SEND children. We need policies around the school environment, such as exams setup, communication strategies and the establishment of quiet spaces, for SEND children to thrive and achieve their full potential.

Protect Bromley Green Spaces
We must actively protect our wildlife which benefits the whole eco system.  We also need to protect the green belt from inappropriate development.  Keep Bromley beautiful!

Freedom pass for pensioners
We must not remove freedom passes for pensioners.  They are already struggling financially.  Let’s support them.

Bromley Football Club
We are season ticket holders at Charlton Athletic (my husband was raised in Blackheath).  Bromley FC is looking great for promotion to League 1.

I am Charles Mulvihill, a resident of Bickley who is motivated to cultivate a fair, inclusive and progressive society, that has green and democratic ideals at its heart, and which is driven by positivity and hope. It is for these reasons that I joined the Green Party at the beginning of 2025, and the last year has shown that the Greens will stand by its strong principles with a commitment to systemic change that has real benefits to the people of this country.

Since moving to the area two years ago, I have been inspired by the actions of local residents to get more involved with taking care of the area, and have been involved with litter picking as a result, but I’m aware that we must do more, and educate more, to ensure that we are building a better Bromley, and a better Britain, together. Real change starts at a local level, and I hope to support local campaigning so that our residents feel listened to, create initiatives that drive pride for the area, and I will communicate Green policies that have a lasting improvement to our Bickley and Sundridge, our Bromley, our home.

I believe that the Green Party offers the boldest, most honest response to the challenges facing Britain, and I’m proud to be a part of the movement.

Like many residents I value the natural character of our neighbourhoods – the ancient oaks, owl calls at night and woodpecker drumming in the morning, the buzz of bumblebees and gentle singing of robins. Nature needs protection from pollution and from loss of green space, and I would do my best to stop any harm being done to it.

My priorities are clear – human health, restoration of nature and protection of all animals.

Ive lived in Bickley and Sundridge for 20 years, and if elected as your councillor, I pledge to be a visible and active representative of our community.

I currently work as a teaching assistant, and I'm about to finish my teacher training. I've previously worked with youths with Special Educational Needs at a holiday club, and served as a youth councillor for 2 years.

Im passionate about the quality of local education and road safety. I live on a road where speeding is prevalent, despite being near 3 schools. I'd like to see measures put in place to prevent this.

Promoted by Bromley and Biggin Hill Labour Party, on behalf of Kyle Sewell, both at 15 Devonshire Square, Bromley, BR2 9HX

I am 78 years old and have been resident in the London Borough of Bromley since 1979 (in Penge). 
I joined Bromley Green Party in 2017, based on the belief in the need to support Nature, and the need to avoid unnecessary consumption involving fossil fuel use and the damage to the Earth's ecosystem through increasing the carbon dioxide load on the atmosphere. We need to avoid waste, especially of plastics which can take millennia to disappear from our environment. We need to nurture our soils, which are being degraded very rapidly, and rebalance our food system. 
To this end I have an allotment which provides healthy food, though obviously I am not self-sufficient. We must reduce fuel-consuming transport; to this end I cycle wherever feasible, otherwise mainly use public transport.

As far as local policies are concerned, I am in favour of 20mph speed limits in built-up areas, mainly to avoid deaths; increasing of taxes on larger, heavier cars, which cause disproportionate damage to road surfaces, problems of congestion and road deaths; construction of public housing at 
affordable rents for people who will never afford purchasing a home, and more effective waste management.

Geoff is retired from the Construction Industry after working on many projects within the Greater London Area. Formerly a committee member of Biggin Hill Residents Association, a Biggin Hill Councillor and Chair of the LBB Planning Committee, he is currently a Trustee of Bromley MENCAP and DUFFUS Centre Foundation. He has a passionate belief that the individual has a vital role to play in their community and that Council decisions must be discussed openly .

Though not born or raised in Biggin Hill, I came here a year ago to pursue my dream career. A hidden gem of a village built on the same determination and unity which inspired me to join the Green Party. Since this village has given me so much in that time, I have decided to give back by standing as a candidate for the council election.

Having spent so much time in the area, I understand the issues that face us all. Bus services that fail to serve, roads that are falling apart, antisocial behaviour on our doorsteps. All while our community is forgotten about, except by developers itching to quite literally dig into our precious landscape and community spaces. As a Biggin Hill councillor I will strive to put these wrongs right, making sure we can all depend on our infrastructure without worrying about more failures, and keeping the beating heart of this village alive. As a Green Party councillor, I will constantly work to uplift the environment as well as the community, preserving our courageous past whilst securing a sustainable future for us all.

As politics on all levels becomes increasingly hostile, now more than ever is the time to bring hope and unity back to our communities, and I will start that right here. By voting Green on 7th May, you can join me in fostering that hope, and we can build a community where nobody is left behind.

Malcolm is a successful local businessman who is a recognised expert in medico-legal law. A retired magistrate, former chairman of Biggin Hill Residents Association, former chairman of Orpington Liberal Democrats and former chairman of Bromley Mencap Appeals Committee. His family as been in Biggin Hill since the 1700s. He stood as a candidate for the Biggin Hill ward in 1978 and 1982.

Alfie Stokes - Reform UK

I stand in Bromley Common & Holwood to serve the community, to protect what we value, and to ensure fair and proper representation across the ward.

As a long-time resident, I care about keeping Bromley Common & Holwood a safe, welcoming place to live, work, and raise a family.

I have proudly served our community for many years, beginning at a young age as a volunteer police cadet. I went on to become a police officer with the Metropolitan Police Service, specialising in Roads and Transport Policing. My broader experience also includes youth policing, with the Volunteer Police Cadets, Safer Neighbourhoods, and Counter Terrorism.

Following my policing career, I continued serving locally in education as a Head of Year, later becoming a Child Protection & Safeguarding Lead. Supporting families and children with special educational needs. Regularly holding agencies to account to ensure vulnerable young people received the support they need.

From day one, my three priorities are clear:

Protecting our Green Belt and open spaces:
I will stand firm against overdevelopment and work to preserve the character, biodiversity, and natural beauty of Bromley.

Improving our roads and road safety:
I will push for higher standards in road maintenance, ensure roadworks are better managed to reduce disruption, and lead targeted campaigns to tackle speeding and improve safety in our neighbourhoods.

Tackling anti-social behaviour and strengthening public safety:
With my background in policing, I understand how to get results. I will work closely with our Safer Neighbourhood Policing Team and local partners to reduce crime and make our streets safer.

On May 7th, vote for all three Reform UK candidates in Bromley Common & Holwood if you want to see practical, common-sense change on Bromley Council.

I’m standing in Bromley Common and Holwood because this borough has been my home since childhood and I care deeply about protecting what makes it a great place to live and work.

Through leading a local charity, working with young people facing mental health challenges, and supporting funding for projects across Bromley, I’ve seen first-hand what matters most to residents.

My leadership roles over the past twenty years’ have all been in organisations focused on improving opportunities for individuals regardless of the challenges they face. And my work in Bromley in sustainability and connecting community over the past 7 years has helped me to truly understand that the best local decisions come from listening carefully to residents, recognising the unique needs of each neighbourhood and planning for the long term.

People here value their community feel, access to local services, neighbourhood character and green spaces. But many residents are concerned about rising costs, traffic and road safety, pressure on local services, and whether they are getting real value from council decisions.

I believe local politics should focus on practical improvements: supporting local centres, making streets safer, creating meaningful opportunities for young people, protecting green spaces, and ensuring that development provides the infrastructure, affordability and sustainability that our community needs.

My priorities would include:
•	Affordable social housing delivered without costing us our biodiversity and important green spaces, and bringing underused/empty property back into use
•	Ensuring young people get the education they need, with the right support provided where there are additional needs
•	Road safety for all, with well-maintained routes and sufficient crossings
•	Giving better support to, and putting the needs of local small businesses, on the council agenda
•	Connecting community across ages and social groups, with real chances to learn from, support and share with

Geraldine Ker
Mum of three, Environmental Scientist and 26 year resident of Bromley Common and Holwood. 16 years volunteering — mentoring young people, community events, school PTA raising thousands of pounds, Havelock Recreation Ground committee. Wants real investment in youth services, green spaces, community programmes and road safety.

I have lived in Bromley all my life (apart from a few years at university in Sheffield) and am passionate about making our ward Bromley Common and Holwood better after years of cuts and threats on our green spaces.

I work in rail and am also a union rep for RMT (National Union of Rail, Maritime and Transport workers) as I’m very passionate about equality, inclusion and improving terms and conditions for all people. I will bring this passion to the council and fight against cuts to our public services we pay for.

I am also an active committee member of Friends of Whitehall recreation ground and have been involved in a lot of fundraising events to improve the park over the years. Our green spaces in our ward are under threat and I will fight any attempt to attack them, especially considering the development that hook farm is being threatened by the recent development proposal.

As a councillor I will represent everyone and make sure that everything I do is in the interests of residents in our ward.

My priorities include: 
• Protecting our valuable green spaces and preventing building on them, and providing actual affordable housing through development of underused/abandoned properties. 
• Keeping Bromley in London so we don’t lose our benefits of free bus travel, and discounted rail travel for under 18s and over 60s. 
• Decrease privatisation of public assets and bring more in-house, saving money and increasing quality of public services. 
• Make our roads safer by lowering speed limit and increase safer road crossings. 
• Giving better support to local independent businesses so that they can not only survive but thrive in Bromley.

Working with fellow Green candidates Clare Searle and Geraldine Kerr, we would represent a real change for our community in Bromley Common & Holwood. Therefore I am asking you to please cast all three votes for your Green Party candidates on 7th May.

Clive was brought up and educated in Orpington, joining the Young Liberals aged 18.

Then in his twenties he moved to Chelsfield with my wife and children, where he lived for many years, running his own business. Six years ago following retirement he moved to Chatterton Village.

Should he be elected he would hope to bring some new ideas and positive opposition to the council.

Julie Ireland is proud to be standing to represent Bromley & Biggin Hill in the 2024 General Election. She has lived in Bromley for over 30 years and raised her two children here. Elected as a councillor for Bromley Town in 2022, Julie is well known locally as a strong voice for the community, leading campaigns for improved air quality, safer roads, affordable housing and making sure that people without online access are not excluded from public services. 

Nationally Julie is deeply concerned by the state of our public services. After recently experiencing a serious health scare, she witnessed first-hand the very real strain on A&E departments as well as the dedication of the NHS staff who treated her. She is passionate about improving healthcare and making sure that everyone has access to the high quality treatment they deserve. 

Julie cares deeply about the local community and would seek to give voice to those who may otherwise struggle to be heard. As a Liberal Democrat she is campaigning for a fair deal to empower communities and put residents first.

Sam has lived in the London Borough of Bromley his whole life. He is married to Vicki and they have 2 young daughters, aged 4 and 2. During his career Sam has worked in travel, advertising and political communications and is currently an Account Manager for a technology company based in London.

Sam first got involved in politics during the 2001 General Election campaign when he met Simon Hughes and canvassed for him in Bermondsey. He was later an intern in Simon’s constituency office for a year before studying Politics at the University of East Anglia in Norwich.

As a frequent commuter to London by train, Sam knows how important it is to continue to fight for improved rail services and local station upgrades in Bromley. During the height of the Covid-19 pandemic Sam coordinated donations from neighbours for a local foodbank and conducted local litter picks.

Sam has called for an overhaul of the Waldo Road Recycling Centre given strong concerns of local residents, with increased traffic seen at the site in recent months and resultant poor air quality reported in the area. Sam has spoken to hundreds of residents across Bromley in recent months to listen and understand their concerns. As a passionate community campaigner Sam is committed to speaking up on behalf of residents who are critical of the height of new developments proposed for Bromley town centre. He is deeply committed to preserving the character of Bromley Town and is a member of the Bromley Civic Society.

My name is Rose, and I'm a 33-year-old Bromley Town resident.

I grew up in West Wickham, and spent much of my teens on the 119 between Bromley and Croydon, before heading off to university, then moving closer to the City. I moved back to the heart of Bromley around four years ago, and you'll often find me in Queensmead, Bromley Palace Park or Beckenham Place Park with my one-eyed rescue dog – he's working on his social skills; I like to think I've already got mine sorted!

I believe Bromley Town is a place worth fighting for. But I also think we can do better – for the people who feel unseen, for the green spaces we're slowly losing, and for the local businesses that give our town its soul.

I care deeply about building a community that is genuinely welcoming and inclusive – where every resident, whatever their background, feels they have a voice and a stake in this town's future. I want to be the voice to help represent us all: listening first, advocating always.

I believe local residents deserve to be able to vote for representation that resonates with their own values – and I believe in showing up for this community regardless.

If you share that belief, I'd be honoured to represent you as a Green Party representative for Bromley Town.

Having come to this country for university nearly 15 years ago, for the last few years, I have
proudly made Bromley my home. Having lived in various parts of the borough I currently live
right in the town centre which is also the ward I am running in. All of us who are Bromley
residents should be proud of living in one of the greenest boroughs in London and should
fight to keep it that way at all costs. Granted, those who work multiple jobs or cut back on
basic necessities to make ends meet this may not find this a priority. I recognize that green
initiatives locally and nationally must go hand in hand with fair and just economic system
which I believe that the Green Party offers. Bromley town centre is beautiful and vibrant, but
we all have seen the devastating effects of drug addiction in the ward especially on the high
street. The current drug policy has failed to address this issue adequately, so I believe that
the Green Party approach of treating drug addiction as a public health issue is the only way
forward. I have previous experience of working in community development outside the
borough and I have first hand experience of seeing the impact that local projects have on the
wellbeing of a community. I am staunchly anti-racist and I have previously volunteered for
anti-racism organizations. I aim to represent all residents of Bromley town, work hard to
maintain our green spaces, improve our social services and guarantee equal access to them
for all residents.

Owen Wittekind has lived in Bromley his whole life, went to school here, and now works in the Borough. Owen has recently started working part-time in education, and said “I can see how important support for young people will be as part of the Covid recovery, be it in catching up on lost learning, but also their need for mental health support”.

Across the board, now more than ever we need leadership and action from our local representatives to deliver for residents. The Cost of Living Crisis has pushed our community over the edge, but the lack of investment and financial support for working families in Mottingham has been going on for years. If elected, Owen will fight to: extend free school meals into the school holidays; give Mottingham residents a voice in decision-making which affects them; and invest in local businesses, community projects and the high street, rather than foreign banks.

Carol is passionate about local politics and both lives and works  in Bromley Town. A resident for 22 years; raising four children here. Carol is a qualified Medicinal Chemist/Drug Repurposer; and Integrative Medicinal Practitioner. Previously, a Research Psychologist; BSc (hons) Psychology; her academic work involved conducting two large  studies in an Islington Primary School -  into the effects of children's cognitions on their appraisal of media violence. This work was  undertaken when she was a senior lecturer at London Met, where she also co-authored an academic text on  "Issues in Applied Developmental Psychology and Child Psychiatry".

Today, she uses her teaching skills (PGCE, MSc Ed) to lead a tutoring business based in Bromley Town. Carol is a well-known and trusted community activist, advocate, and campaigner; championing local issues and providing advocacy and advice for Bromley residents. Carol is keen to create a Bromley Town that is safer, cleaner, and far more cultured and vibrant. Carol feels that if she is elected as a Bromley Town councillor,  she can work collegiately with the other parties to achieve sustained improvement.

Caroline has lived in Bromley for 22 years and is passionate about enhancing adult social care. Caroline has a wealth of experience and post graduate qualifications pertaining to the health and care sector; working in the NHS for 30 years - across a range of services; such as general nursing, acute mental health and mother and baby units. At present, she is a director and manager of a CQC-registered domiciliary care agency. Anyone who knows Caroline can bear testimony to her focused delivery of compassionate support to vulnerable residents;  driving positive change in the London Borough of Bromley. Indeed, Caroline would like to do far more - representing the good people of Bromley Town on the council.

Sumathy (Sum) has been a Bromley Resident for  23 years. A dedicated, civic-minded individual with extensive business experience; previously owning and running a chain of newsagents in the City of London. Sum is now keen to contribute to local initiatives and wants to use her talents in  horticulture and crafts to help launch one of  Better Bromley's initiatives; creating spaces for elderly and vulnerable residents to gain therapeutic benefit from both crafts and gardening. Indeed, Sum now has the time and commitment to make a positive impact in Bromley Town as your representative on the Council.

Please see more information on Alison Stammers at 

https://www.chislehurstmatters.com/championing-chislehurst/who-we-are/alison-stammers/

Sometimes it’s very easy to sit on the side-line, express a view and let life go by – I don’t want to be one of these people – We the residents of Chislehurst deserve better – let’s make the change!

I strongly believe that we need to make Chislehurst, the place that everyone has chosen to live in, live up to everyone’s expectations for themselves and their families.  Local residents deserve the best from this fantastic town in which they live, and they need to have their voices heard, listened to, and acted upon.

We have a huge opportunity to change the norm, to change the way we do things in Chislehurst – to put a stop to the Punch and Judy style of politics, to elect councillors who don’t see your vote as another step up the career ladder – who care about local issues, not party politics – who are involved in the nuts and bolts of our community –  to elect councillors who listen, are passionate and engaged, but who also have a track record of getting things done. Its a very exciting time in Chislehurst but we cant do anything without you!  .

Together we can give Chislehurst the representation and voice it deserves.

I have lived in Chislehurst since 1998 and have been a member of the Labour Party since 1992. I work at EY in London Bridge. I am actively involved in the Chislehurst May Queen Society and a sponsor of Chislehurst Rocks. I am also an active parent member of the fundraising campaign at Newstead Wood School

Laura McCracken holds UK and US citizenship. She's lived and worked in cities around the world - including London, New York, Singapore, Luxembourg, Munich and Los Angeles - but she's now made Bromley her home.

Laura has built a successful career in the fintech industry and has a deep passion for economics, history, politics and international relations.

"After a 30 year career in international business, I'm ready to give back to the community I now call home. If elected, my priorities will be to protect what makes Bromley special, to have the courage to speak up when I see something isn't right, and to support the incredible local groups who work tirelessly to help vulnerable people and look after our community spaces."

Laura was previously a member of the Conservative Party but became disillusioned over its role in Brexit. More recently she has been deeply concerned by the party's silence in the face of rising populism.

"At heart I've always believed in the values of liberal democracy. I joined the Liberal Democrats because their values are my values."

Jeremy Adams is a Dad of three and a Clock House resident of 18 years. He served as a governor at Balgowan Primary School from 2014-21 and will campaign to invest in Bromley’s community and services, including education support services and safer roads for cyclists. Jeremy’s a former journalist and now runs a training business.

Josh was born and went to school in Bromley. He returned to Bromley 15 years ago and has lived in Clock House since then.

Having stood for election as a Clock House councillor in 2014 and narrowly missing election, he continued to work with Councillors Ian Dunn and Vanessa Allen to campaign on behalf of local residents. This led to him being elected in May 2018 alongside Ian and Vanessa.

Josh sits on the council’s Industrial Relations sub-committees, the Development Control Committee and the General Purposes and Licensing Committee.

Like Vanessa, Josh was actively involved in the campaign against the demolition of Beckenham Library and used his position as the only Labour councillor on the Renewal, Recreation and Housing Committee to vote against these proposals and spoke against them at the Council Executive.

More recently, Josh has worked with local businesses to support them in securing council funding for the parade of shops on Elmers End Road.

I have lived and grown up in Beckenham my entire life and I have many fond memories of the area. Our community is what makes us special. The familiar faces at local cafes and pubs, the chippy down the road and other restaurants and local shops that make the area feel like home. Politics has become too much about shouting the loudest and driving a divisive agenda. I’m not here to shout; I’m here to listen and get things done, and if elected, I’ll do exactly that.
I am immensely proud to be one of the youngest candidates running in this election and supporting young people throughout the borough is a priority of mine. As someone who has recently left school and moved to pursue my undergraduate degree in law, I remember my time in education well. If elected, I will ensure that schools across the borough are properly supported, especially SEND students. Almost a fifth of pupils in Bromley have SEND; I’d push for greater accessibility inside and out of the classroom and ensure support for both students, teachers and parents. Likewise, outside of school I want to ensure adequate investment and support into youth services, after-school programmes and safe spaces.
I am also too familiar now with rising rent prices and unaffordable housing, an imploding job market and a world torn apart by climate change; all burdens that young people now face. Yet, the cost of living crisis affects us all. I plan to support all residents through the cost of living crisis and pushing for genuinely affordable and accessible social housing. I’d also push to make Bromley a ‘living wage’ borough, promoting the living wage rather than minimum wage through all council employment, partnerships and contracts.
Residents constantly bring up crime as a massive issue in the area. I’d liaise with local authorities and residents to input schemes to tackle crime and anti-social behaviour, reduce shoplifting and increase safety on the streets. Likewise, I’d work to tackle graffiti and vandalism across our c

Ruth McGregor grew up in South East London and has lived locally with her family for over 30 years. An accountant by profession, she has worked in a number of different organisations, including some years at Transport for London, and has a keen interest in public transport as well as the environment. For the last few years she has been working in the charity sector which has opened her eyes to the inequalities in society and the need to resolve this.

Ryan is restanding as local Councillor to continue improving everyday life and equality within our community.

He is proud to be fighting and delivering for our residents, businesses and local charities across the ward and Borough!

If re-elected, he will continue to help drive more investment into the local economy and crucial council services.

I was born, grew up and have lived in the local area for most of my life, and I care deeply about the future of our community.

As a new mum, a neurodivergent woman, and someone from a minoritised ethnic background, I know how often people feel unheard. Through my work as a lawyer, coach and advocate, I’ve built a career around listening to people, supporting them, and standing up for them when it matters.

I’m standing to make our roads safer, tackle issues like fly-tipping and housing standards, and make sure residents are properly heard at Bromley Council. Our area deserves councillors who listen, act, and stand up for their community.

I grew up in Penge and have lived in Crystal Palace since 2014, so this area is home to me. I am now ready to give back to the community that helped shape me and my children. 
I hold a Master’s degree in Politics and an undergraduate degree in Education and Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND). I am also a keynote speaker and an advocate for youth engagement, environmental issues, and SEND support, motivated by my experience as a parent of a child with SEND.
As a mum to children in local schools in the ward, I care deeply about the future of our community. I want to ensure Bromley council allocates better funding for safer streets, better maintained green spaces, and real opportunities for young people and those with disabilities to be heard, seen, learn and thrive.

Ann Garrett is a candidate for Bromley Town, a performing arts tutor for Adult Learning Lewisham, and has been a Green Party member for over 20 years. She is secretary of Bromley CND, convenor of Bromley Peace Council, joint co-ordinator of Bromley FoE, and a member of Amnesty, Bromley Civic Society, and TUC Pensioners Action Association. Recently she has organised local Cop26 climate awareness performances and as part of this is involved in campaigning to urge Bromley Council to declare a climate emergency and reduce pollution. Creating local green new jobs and more social housing are also priorities.

As a young local resident who has lived in Farnborough and Crofton all my life, I have seen first-hand how our communities and our country have changed over the years. Like many local people, I have watched standards slip, local voices go unheard, and too many residents feel ignored by those elected to represent them. I can no longer stand by while our communities are asked to accept lower standards and weaker local leadership. That is why I have decided to step forward and represent the people of Farnborough and Crofton.

Through my work supporting local residents, businesses, and community initiatives, I have seen both the challenges we face and the incredible potential our area still holds. I believe Farnborough and Crofton can become an example for the rest of the country, where strong communities, thriving local businesses, protected green spaces, safe streets, and well-maintained roads are not ambitions, but everyday realities.

I want to see our villages flourish, our local trade supported, our streets kept clean, our roads properly maintained, and our green belt protected for future generations. I want our older generation to receive the dignity, care, and respect they have earned, and I want younger families to feel proud and confident raising their children in a community built on strong values, neighbourly spirit, and local pride.

I believe leadership starts with listening, showing up, and being accountable. You will not just hear from me at election time, you will see me on our streets, in our local businesses, at resident meetings, and tackling problems where they happen. I will be visible, approachable, and committed to working alongside residents to deliver practical solutions that genuinely improve everyday life.

Together, we can restore pride, strengthen community, and build a future that puts Farnborough and Crofton first.

Born here. Raised here. Ready to serve here.

Ian lives in Orpington and works in the insurance sector in the City of London.

Born in Scotland, Ian has travelled extensively and lived and worked in a number of different countries before settling in Orpington with his wife. He is a keen believer in working to improve his local community and believes that Petts Wood & Knoll deserves hard working councillors.

Ian is especially interested in protecting local health services, making our roads safer and ensuring that local businesses can continue to thrive.

Vote Ian in Petts Wood & Knoll for a a Liberal Voice on Bromley Council!

I am proud to be the third generation of my family to call the Bromley borough home. Although I live just outside the Farnborough and Crofton ward (so close that the houses at the bottom of my garden fall within it) I walk through the ward every day with my dog and feel deeply connected to this community.

Since moving here in 2019, I’ve seen first‑hand how our neighbourhood supports one another, especially during the most difficult moments of the COVID pandemic. That resilience and sense of care inspired me to become more active locally, including through my involvement in the Women’s Institute. I also make a point of supporting our independent traders, who are such a vital part of what makes this area vibrant and unique.

I know that everyday people in our community continue to face rising living costs and growing pressures. I am standing for election because I want to help ensure that local voices are heard, that our neighbourhood remains a place where families can thrive, and that we continue to look out for one another—just as we did when times were toughest. If I am elected, three principles will guide everything I do: making life more affordable, backing the caring majority, and protecting our environment for the future.

I was born and brought up on Farnborough Village prior to moving to the Medway Towns for 10 years now a resident in Crofton for the past 27 years.

I am The Director of a Social Care Company providing care and support to people living in the Orpington area. I have three grown up Children and 6 Grandchildren living in America and Frankfurt so a lot of my holidays is spent visiting family

I am a member and a serving Elder at Christ Church URC in Petts Wood. In my spare time I enjoy walking along the Beach in Camber and Gardening

I have been a Farnborough & Crofton resident for almost 20 years, and I believe it is time to stand up and influence changes at our Bromley Council. As a father of two children who went to local schools and a member of those schools’ parents association, I feel the urgent need to actually do something about our cost of living crisis we have faced for too long and it has not been dealt with by those in charge who were in position to make a change but never did.

I hear constantly from residents about the unbearable pollution and noise caused by choked roads, old diesel buses, and local air traffic in particular, in our wonderful and green belt, Farnborough & Crofton.

As Biggin Hill Airport Limited has dramatically increased local air traffic, resulting in significant noise pollution affecting our community. I will therefore campaign for the introduction of a fair taxation or landing fee on private jet travel for BHAL to help mitigate the noise and environmental impact on our community and to finance lower council tax, to support those families who struggle with their utility bills and the cost of living, and increase our police force too.

An often quoted issue and my personal desire from residents is the need for more sustainability. We need urgent support for our local repair shops, second-hand, and other small-shops that keep disappearing/closing in our high street due to rising utility bills and cost of living crisis.

We, the Bromley Green Party, will be a strong voice for your demands, your freedom, your health, your green spaces and be a reliable supporter for cleaner air and fairer living for EVERYONE.

I was born at the PRUH in 2000, and have lived locally all my life.

I attended Wickham Common Primary School in Coney Hall, and Hayes School in Hayes. I later attended the University of Warwick and graduated in 2022 with a degree in Politics and Sociology.

I now work in Central London at CCHQ as a Media Monitoring Officer.

Having grown up locally, I am heavily invested in this wonderful area. I previously volunteered at the West Wickham and Shirley Baptist Church at their Sunday School, and later as a Befriending Volunteer for Age UK.

I have been involved in the Local Conservatives since age 15, campaigning on issues such as unfair cuts to our local train services.

Now, I am looking forward to working with my Local Conservative colleagues across Bromley to make sure that Hayes, Coney Hall and Keston continue to be places where everyone has the opportunity to live, work, play and grow up in a safe, secure and beautiful Borough – just as I did.

Aside from politics, I enjoy travelling, spending time with my family and, when time allows, making the most of Bromley and Central London’s nightlife.

Alexa was born in 1969 and has lived in Bromley all her life. She was educated in Bromley and holds degrees from the University of East Anglia and University College London. A qualified librarian, Alexa runs the information service at the London Chamber of Commerce and Industry in the City of London.

She represented Bromley Common & Keston Ward between 1994 and 2022. Since 2022, Alexa has represented Hayes & Coney Hall. She is well known locally and has a reputation for getting things done.

Her main interests are planning, the environment and policing. She strongly believes that Green Belt and other designated land must be protected from built development and that local planning authorities should have more power to resist building on unsuitable sites. She wants all residents to be able to live in a clean, safe environment with visible police patrols.

As a daily commuter into central London, Alexa believes that reliable, clean and safe public transport is essential, both for the benefit of individuals and the wider economy.

Alexa currently chairs the Development Control Committee on Bromley Council. She also sits on several other committees, including Plans 1 Sub-Committee, the Public Protection & Enforcement Scrutiny Committee, the Children, Education and Families Scrutiny Committee and General Purposes and Licensing Committee.

Outside of politics, Alexa is a keen traveller and has visited many countries, including Arctic Norway, Greenland and Iceland. When time allows, Alexa participates in classes in the Borough’s excellent Mytime Active sports centres. She enjoys history and is a member of the Royal Society of St George, a non-political society for all people who share a love of England and all things English. Alexa also enjoys team quizzes and is very competitive!

Diane has lived in Hayes for over 42 years and has been a Councillor in Kelsey and Eden Park since 2010. This is the area where she grew up.

A pupil at Balgowan School in Beckenham and then Langley Park Grammar School for Girls, Diane qualified as a physiotherapist from the Royal London Hospital in 1981. She spent 27 years working in the NHS in and around Bromley. During her professional career in the health service, Diane held both clinical and senior managerial roles, so she brings a wealth of experience to her current Cabinet position at Bromley Council as Executive Portfolio Holder for Adult Care and Health.

Since taking up her Cabinet post, the Council have won three national awards, two with NHS colleagues. These relate to hospital discharge and a homeless project, and one in recognition of Bromley Council’s groundbreaking work in tackling loneliness in the Borough. This is now being looked at with a view to supporting policy development in Continental Europe.

I am a retired Assistant Head teacher who worked for 32 years. I have lived in Coney Hall for 30 years and know the area very well. My children attended Wickham Common Primary School and Ravenswood School.

I believe in the worth and power of the local community. As such, I was a member of the Wickham Court Players at the Assembly Halls before it disbanded. When I retired, I joined the committee of the Residents Association, volunteered to read with children at Wickham Common School and became a Brownie leader. I am the secretary of Friends of Coney Hall Park and a Well Wood volunteer. I am passionate about keeping our local area beautiful and preserving the green belt which we know is now under threat from development in Bromley.  I accept that we need to have affordable housing for our young people and our key workers but any developments should be sensitive and appropriate for the area and on brown belt land.

I have never been afraid to speak out about things that matter to me such as the loss of biodiversity and social injustice. I am the founder of Environmental Action Coney Hall which has won four awards not only for its environmental impact but also for community action. We held a wassail in the community orchard in February and will be running our third open gardens event this June. To date we have raised £1,200 for charity.

I am a hopeful, optimistic person, prepared to listen to alternative views and concerns. I want a better future for our young people. I support the Green Party not only because environmental concerns and social justice are the core principles of its policy but also because it is offering a viable, positive alternative voice which does not seek to divide us as a community by instilling fear and using hateful rhetoric.

Hi, I’m Major Matt Aldridge – local dad, Army veteran and clean energy expert.

My family has lived right here in Salisbury for generations. From doing ‘bob a job’ as a Scout to serving as Head Boy of Bishop Wordsworth’s School, chalk runs in my veins.

As a father to a young daughter, I know how hard it is to raise a family in a cost-of-living crisis and what it’s going to take to turn our economy around.

As an army officer who served on the frontline in Afghanistan, I can rally morale, engage national leaders and get tough things done.

I’m registered partially-sighted, so I’ve lived the frustrations of accessibility — and successfully campaigned for change.

Finally, as an experienced civil servant and clean energy strategist, I know how to navigate Westminster. I’ll fight to bring Salisbury a brighter future, with an economy that works as hard as you do. Where local business can thrive.

Where our rivers are clean and our farms are climate-resilient. And our historic city is celebrated and supported to thrive. 

That’s why I’m standing up as your committed and energetic Labour MP,  who is always happy to listen and proud to put Salisbury first.

Ju has several years of casework experience giving her a strong understanding of the issues people may need support with. She studied International Politics and Military History at University. She has previously worked in retail and enjoys volunteering in the local community.

Ju will stand up for the residents Kelsey and Eden Park and be a strong representative on Bromley Council if elected, regularly engaging with residents.

I’m a lifelong resident of the Kelsey & Eden Park ward, a local mum with children in the same schools I once attended, and the daughter of parents who still live in the family home where I grew up. That connection across generations gives me a clear understanding of the issues local families face every day.

I run a successful business in Beckenham, which gives me practical insight into the challenges experienced by local enterprises.

I care deeply about family, our community, and our country’s future. My roots, experience, and commitment to this area shape everything I do.

I’m dedicated to listening, working hard, and helping our neighbourhood return to being a place we’re proud to call home. I’m standing for Bromley Council to deliver practical solutions, transparent decision‑making, and a strong, accountable voice for our community.

I have lived in and been part of this community all my life, and I was educated in a local school in the ward. Having grown up here, I understand the importance of creating a safe and supportive environment for young people. I am committed to being a strong and passionate voice for the younger generation, working closely with our Safer Neighbourhood Police Team to help ensure they feel secure, valued, and supported within the community.

I am equally committed to protecting our valued green spaces, fighting for lower council tax, and always putting the residents of Kelsey & Eden Park ward first in every decision I make.

I’ve lived in the Borough for over 35 years and run my own business within the building industry. In recent years, I’ve witnessed a depletion of services, vanity projects with no public benefit, increased costs for motorists to the detriment of local businesses, and large-scale development proposals that harm local communities. Bromley residents now pay more and receive less, and still the Council forecasts that Bromley will go bust by 2028. Residents of Kelsey and Eden Park deserve better. A vote for Reform UK will deliver that.

I have lived in Eden Park for 34 years. Now I’m retired I have the time to help Bromley become even better. If elected, I will enjoy meeting my neighbours and representing your concerns at Council meetings.

I grew up in Bromley, attended Bromley Grammar School for Boys, and have always lived in south east London. I have chaired charities in Southwark and Coventry helping people affected by unemployment, poverty or homelessness. I chair Petts Wood and Orpington Chess Club, Kent’s largest chess club with a thriving junior section. I am married and have four daughters and six grandchildren.

As a business owner for almost 30 years and then as a charity UK Operations Manager, my core values are efficiency, transparency, communication, and collaboration.

I joined the Green Party because I am deeply concerned about the effects of Brexit, years of austerity and immigration policies which have weakened public services such as the NHS. The Green Party stands to levy a 1% tax on those with over £10m, and 2% on those with over £100m. This will produce between £15bn–£25bn per year which will strengthen the economy and relieve the tax burden on the rest of us. I am also very concerned about the rise of the far-right, and am passionate about our ‘Hope not Hate’ message.

We have a huge commitment to the environment. We want clean air so children do not develop asthma because of traffic pollution, and sewage-free water so children can swim safely in the sea and in our rivers. Water companies have extracted billions in profits while failing to invest in infrastructure. We want them brought back into public ownership.

I am actively involved with Bromley’s Green Party and whatever the result of this election, will continue to ensure that we maintain Bromley’s reputation as ‘Clean and Green’ and one of the best London boroughs.

These are my main interests and priorities:
keeping Bromley Clean and Green: reducing flytipping and littering, increasing recycling, planting trees
protec

Having lived in Kelsey and Eden Park ward my entire life, I am deeply familiar with the concerns of local residents. Since joining the Green Party last year, I have been proud to represent the interests of young people and families under a banner I trust to stand up for ordinary people and reflect their voices in decision-making.

As your representative on Bromley Council, I would prioritise road safety, particularly on Wickham Road and Upper Elmers End Road, which have become hotspots for dangerous driving. I would advocate the introduction of 20mph speed limits on the most dangerous roads, ensuring that Bromley aligns with the 2022 changes to the Highway Code, which place greater responsibility on motorists and prioritise pedestrians at crossings and junctions. Fatal and life-changing accidents have no place on our streets, and I would work to reduce the risks on our roads.

As a volunteer at Dorset Road Allotments, I understand the value of protecting our green spaces, including parks and nature reserves. I would campaign for renewed investment in Kelsey Park, reversing years of Conservative neglect. The closure of the main bridge since 2021, with restoration works only commencing in January 2026, is emblematic of wider challenges facing Bromley: continued service reductions alongside repeated council tax increases.

I believe the time has come to seek change at council level, guided by the Green Party's commitment to an equitable society, where working people can thrive rather than feeling the pinch.

I've lived in the London Borough of Bromley for the last 2.5 years, and have quickly fallen in love with this beautiful area and felt part of the community.

Living in this area, I have quickly become aware of the issues facing Mottingham, and Bromley more widely, that have been left unaddressed for far too long by the Conservative run council. Speed limits in our neighbourhoods remain dangerously high, many suffer in precarious housing situations, our high streets are deteriorating, and low-level crime often goes unaddressed. At the heart of this is a failure to invest in our communities.

As Mottingham’s Green Party representative, I will work hard to address these concerns. A core principle of the Green Party is protecting our environment and green spaces. As a former volunteer of Friends of the Earth and Chair of my company's Planet Group, I understand the importance of ensuring that polluters are held accountable, in order to protect local areas.

Our priorities in the Green Party in Mottingham go beyond environmental issues. Within Bromley we recognise that there are increasing housing issues and we will work hard to ensure that action to tackle rising homelessness, to provide affordable, sustainably developed housing as well as to provide stronger support for disability, mental health and addiction services within the borough. We will also continue pressing for greater funding for local government, so our communities receive the support they deserve.

Our slogan is Real Hope, Real Change. Josh and I are here to prove that Really Works.

Hi, I’m Josh Thomas, and alongside Jamie Tulacz, I’m running to be one of Mottingham’s Green Party councillors.

I’m a 22-year-old student of History, Philosophy and Politics at Lancaster University, and I previously studied abroad at the University of Toronto. Outside of my studies, I’ve been a lifelong resident of the London Borough of Bromley. I grew up just a short bus ride from Mottingham on the 273, and the area has always felt like part of my local community.

Growing up locally means I’m acutely aware of the issues facing Mottingham, and Bromley more widely, that have been left unaddressed for far too long by the establishment parties. Speed limits in our neighbourhoods remain dangerously high, our high streets are deteriorating, and low-level crime often goes unaddressed. At the heart of this is a failure to invest in our communities.

As Mottingham’s Green Party representative, I will work tirelessly to address these concerns. A core principle of the Green Party is protecting our environment and green spaces. As co-founder of Bromley and Sevenoaks Greenpeace, I understand the importance of holding polluters accountable and protecting local areas like Marvels Wood from fly-tipping, dog-fouling, and neglect.

But our priorities go beyond the environment. There can be no climate justice without social and economic justice. Jamie and I will push for affordable, sustainably developed housing, action to reduce rising homelessness, and stronger support for disability, mental health and addiction services - borough wide. We will also continue pressing for greater funding for local government, so our communities receive the support they deserve.

Our slogan is Real Hope, Real Change. Jamie and I are here to prove that Really Works.

I’m standing to represent Orpington Ward because this is my home — it always has been. 
 
I was born here, I’ve lived here all my 48 years, and I’ve raised my three children here. Two of them are still in local education, so what happens in this community matters to me — it affects my family every single day.
 
I’ve spent most of my working life in Orpington too. I’ve managed retail businesses locally and still do — I don’t just shop on the high street, I work in it. Over the years, I’ve employed and worked alongside countless local residents, and I’m proud to have given many young people their first step into work. 
 
I’m not a career politician — and I won’t pretend to be. What I do have is real-world experience, a deep understanding of the area, and a genuine connection to the people who live here. Like many of you, I’ve grown increasingly frustrated with the direction things are heading — both nationally and locally. Too often, it feels like ordinary people are ignored while common sense takes a back seat. 
 
That’s why I’ve decided to stand. Not for a title, but to do something about it. I want to be a strong, local voice on Bromley Council — someone who understands Orpington, listens to its residents, and isn’t afraid to speak up for them.

A fourth-generation Orpington resident; I am proud to call this area home.  I am passionate about preserving Orpington’s character as a hidden gem with easy access to London and the Borough's unique countryside. After starting as a structural engineer, I built a career in financial services. I have previously served as a local councillor in Bromley from 2010 - 2022, and as Chairman of the planning sub-committee, I have a proven track record of protecting our green belt from inappropriate development. I am particular concerned about the threat of high-rise development in the high-street and the Council's development in Priory Gardens. I will work hard as being a strong voice for you all.

Allan Tweddle is the Liberal Democrat London Assembly candidate for Bexley and Bromley.

Allan grew up in Hartlepool but has lived in south-east London since 1998. He is a qualified chartered accountant, a governor at his local secondary school and a volunteer speaker for the Guide Dogs For The Blind Association. He is married, with two children.

Though he has always been interested in politics he only joined the Liberal Democrats in 2015 when it became clear to him that we could no longer take our Liberal, open, tolerant society for granted.

Allan said: "As a blind person I am constantly amazed by how helpful, supportive and thoroughly genuine my fellow Londoners are, whatever their political persuasion. I'm standing for the London Assembly because I want to give something back to this great city and to all those Londoners who have welcomed me as one of their own."

I have represented Penge and Cator as a Ward Councillor for 12 years and I still have the enthusiasm, time, energy, and commitment to continue for the next 4 years. Having lived in Penge for over 50 years I know the ward well and am engaged with most of the local community groups.

However, very few of our local organisations represent the community we live in. Penge and Cator, although one of the most diverse wards in Bromley does engage enough with residents with disabilities, BAME, and LGBT+ I want to encourage change within these groups, so that all our residents’ views and voices are heard.

Having served on most of the Policy Development and Scrutiny Committees and I am hoping to focus on the serious environmental issues we are all facing. We need bold environmental policies across the borough which should include all LBB contractors.

As a Bromley opposition councillor I speak up for the vulnerable, campaign against cuts to services imposed by Conservative controlled council, and have proved that I am able to put my words into action within the community to ensure no-one is left behind.

Simon has lived in Penge and Cator ward since 2010 as was elected as a Councillor for the ward in 2018. He is currently Deputy Labour of the Bromley Labour Group and sits on the Executive, Resources and Commissioning, Education Children’s and Families and Pensions committees.

Simon is Chair of Penge Partners, the group of volunteers who organise the annual Penge Festival and other community events.

He also volunteers as a trustee for three local charities: Bromley Relief in Need (which provides small grants for those struggling to provide essential items like cookers, beds and fridges) Bromley Experts by Experience (a user led deaf and disabled people’s organisation) and the newly launched Journey to Freedom Fund (which supports Bromley and Croydon Women’s Aid in providing emergency items for women and children leaving refuges having fled domestic abuse.)

He also volunteers as Vice Chair of the Civilian Committee for 1903 Penge and Beckenham Air Cadet Squadron, as a Team Leader for Bromley Homeless Shelter, a mentor for University of Greenwich Business School students and until recently as a volunteer tutor in English for the Access Project and supporting the Friends of Crystal Palace Park Dinosaurs.

Outside of Council and Community work, Simon works part time in a Senior Compliance role for an Asset Management Firm.

Simon came out as bi during a full council meeting in 2019, while proposing a motion supporting LGBT+ Inclusive sex and relationship education in Bromley schools. He organised Bromley Labour’s first ever Pride event and supports organisations such as GiveOUT- which provide funding for LGBT+ groups in countries around the world that advocate and campaign for LGBT+ rights.

He is also a passionate supporter of the climate strike movement and campaigning for emissions reduction, he has marched with and provided advice and training to local climate striker students from Bromley Schools. He proposed that Bromley Council declare climate emergency (which

I have been a Councillor in Penge and Cator since 2014 and have represented Bromley Labour Group on Environment, Adult Services and Children Education and Families Scrutiny committees. I have also been on Development and Control and spent eight years on Planning. Some achievements include securing Melvin Hall as a Community Centre, ensuring the Post Office in Penge was retained and gaining more youth mentoring in Bromley schools at an early age. I am Co-Organiser of Pengefest, Vice Chair of Penge Festival and a member of Penge Forum and various Friends Groups. If elected in May I will continue to hold the Council to account, push initiatives through on behalf of residents and continue as a community activist working with stakeholders and businesses to promote Penge

I am from Brighton but have lived in South London for the past decade. I have a history of voluntary community roles and activism shaped by my own experiences and a passion for positive change. I am now hoping to bring these skills, and my enthusiasm, to representing the Green Party and the residents of Penge and Cator on Bromley Council.

Having grown up in a low income family, I attended a state school in special measures and benefited from schemes such as Sure Start. My work in Youth Centres across London (many since closed during austerity) increased my understanding of the impact made by social programs and I am passionate about maintaining and building practical support for young people both in education and the community.

During the Covid pandemic I co-ordinated a group of several hundred stitchers in South London making scrubs for local hospitals. I am deeply concerned about cuts to an already underfunded health system and the privatisation of essential services.

I have experience working with diverse and marginalised people, previously volunteering at a Red Cross Destitution Centre for Refugees and regularly making and donating costumes to charities working with displaced children. I would advocate for  better support for these underserved communities locally and continue to protest the ongoing crises in Palestine and beyond.

My key concerns at a local level are affordable housing and public transport, green spaces and ensuring adequate support for the elderly, disabled, families on low incomes, and other isolated or marginalised groups.

On a national level I am staunchly anti-austerity and aligned with the Green Party’s policies on environmental action, taxing the rich, and embracing the beauty and value in all of our diverse communities.

Penge and the surrounding area is all I know. I’ve lived here all my life along with three generations of my wider family that make up the strong Caribbean community in South East London. I know our community’s potential, but I’ve also seen how years of the same old politics have let us down. While our current representatives have been in post for a long time, the issues we face aren’t getting better—from the lack of fire safety accountability in new builds and the cycle of pothole-ridden roads, to the rising crime driven by financial inequality.

I’m standing for the Green Party because I’m tired of seeing our pockets and vital public services squeezed by cost cutting, while the top 1% live in abundance. Real change starts at the local level. As your local councillor, I will bring my skillset in accountability and rigour as a software developer to Bromley Council.

For you and for a better Penge, I will:

● Support social housing: Having been disappointed by and objecting to the initial proposed Blenheim development with only 30% of "affordable" housing, I welcome the change to make it 100% social housing. More housing is essential in Penge and throughout London, and this development can have other benefits for our area. However I only support them if they are built to the highest safety standards without cutting corners for profit.
● Fix our infrastructure: I will advocate for brick-style paving rather than tarmac on residential streets as a smarter, long-term investment that ends the cycle of temporary pothole patches and naturally slows traffic.
● Tackle crime at the root: I will push for community-led prevention and rehabilitative schemes that address inequality and reduce reoffending.
● Restore our streets: I will push for more bins and better support for our waste collection teams to tackle litter and fly-tipping properly.

I have proudly called this part of South London home for more than fifteen years, and it is where I have chosen to build both my professional and family life. As the headteacher of a local school, every day I see how strong communities help children and families thrive. The relationships between schools, families,  local organisations and the council are vital, and I am passionate about helping those connections grow even stronger.

Community has always been at the heart of what matters to me. Since welcoming my son in Autumn 2024, the local amenities in Penge and Cator ward have become even more important in our daily life. Like many local parents, I now spend plenty of time enjoying the fantastic resources on our doorstep — visiting the library, playing in our local parks, and taking part in activities at Community Vision. These spaces, which bring people together and make our neighbourhood such a special place to live, need championing and I feel confident to do this at a council level.

Through my work in education, I have extensive experience working closely with local authorities. As a headteacher I regularly collaborate with council services and sit on a number of Local Authority committees, giving me a strong understanding of how local government works and the confidence to navigate the system effectively. I know how decisions are made and how to advocate for the needs of our community.

I first became involved with the Green Party more than twenty years ago because I believe in building fairer, greener and more supportive communities. I love living and working here, and I want to help ensure our area continues to improve — from investing in spaces such as Alexandra Recreation Ground and Cator Park to pushing for step-free access at our local stations. I would be proud to represent our community and work with residents to make our neighbourhood even better.

New to politics, I want to be a strong voice for residents who feel ignored. I'll work to tackle potholes and dangerous pavements, stop HGVs cutting through minor roads, and speed up roadworks to avoid gridlock.

Having lived in Petts Wood & Knoll all my life, this community means everything to me. As a young candidate, I bring a fresh perspective and the energy to stand up for our area and its future. Through my work in the transport and infrastructure sector, I understand the importance of reliable and efficient services.

I know the everyday concerns residents face, and I will fight to help bring down the cost of living, improve safety on our streets, support local businesses, ease pressure on local services and ensure that your voices are heard. It’s time for practical, common-sense leadership that delivers real results for our ward, our borough and our country.

A local resident for over forty years I am passionate about giving residents a strong voice on Bromley Council.
As a self-employed professional with extensive community volunteering experience I will challenge waste, stand up for small businesses and push for fairer central government funding to help reduce Council Tax.

I have been a Bromley resident for nearly 20 years where I live with my husband and three daughters who attend local primary and secondary schools. I teach psychology and criminology at a number of UK universities. Lifelong learning is very close to my heart, and I am currently studying for a degree in Environmental Sciences (partly to equip myself with facts regarding the increasing misinformation spread largely by fossil fuel industries regarding the climate crisis).

Since my youth, I have been active and involved in a range of campaign groups and organisations (stop racism and anti-fascism groups, Amnesty International, Bromley for Europe etc.)  As part of the Green Party, we campaigned for 20mph outside schools and safer streets, against the expansion of Biggin Hill airport, and supported efforts for a second confirmatory Brexit referendum. Being an active member in the community, I was also one of the founding members of “Friends of Widmore Green Spaces”, thus know about the need and benefits of community groups.

My overall current concerns relate to deep structural inequalities in our society and how any austerity measures and “cuts” disproportionally affect the poor and most disadvantaged. This is reflected also at council level. Any problems in our society are blamed on immigrants or refugees, the disabled or any other marginalised group rather than looking at the actual causes of these problems (reckless gambling by financial institutions, Brexit, the “mini budget”, decades long underfunding of public services etc.). This divisive narrative is detrimental to our society and community cohesion, and I strongly reject these views.

The climate crisis is inextricably linked with social justice as those disadvantaged will be disproportionally affected by any climate impacts.
The following aims are some of my priorities:
•	Listen to residents’ concerns
•	Accurate and comprehensive monitoring of air pollution (across the borough including Biggin Hill airport)

Ben is a second-year university student who has lived in Bromley all his life and attended local schools. He is the Bromley Liberal Democrats youth representative and is passionate about issues affecting young people, including crime, employment and public transport. Ben is also committed to protecting local green spaces in Petts Wood & Knoll. He is standing to ensure young people’s voices are heard and to support a greener, safer local community.

I am a retired civil servant and have lived in the area for over 40 years. I have many years' experience representing colleagues as a trade union representative and believe my experience would prove useful in representing Petts Wood & Knoll residents.

I’m married with 3 grown up children and 5 grandchildren. I grew up and have lived in the Orpington area all my adult life and my family all live locally.
