I'm standing as a candidate for Reform UK in the 7 May local elections in Plaistow Ward, Bromley.

With 30 years’ experience in IT and finance, I have previously served as a Bromley Councillor (2018-2022), which I enjoyed immensely giving back to my community. I served as Vice Chairman of both the Pensions Committee and the Renewal, Recreation and Housing Committee, and also worked locally with residents in improving the area and standing up for our community.

I am committed to fiscal competence via oversight and scrutiny of Bromley’s expenditure, standing up strongly for Plaistow residents, and working with local Police in keeping our borough safe.

I will support local Plaistow businesses, and ensure Bromley is a great place for young people to grow up, learn, start careers, and raise a family. I strongly value preserving Bromley’s green spaces, which make Bromley such a fantastic place to live.

“Hi, I’m Mary Ion and I’m running to be your representative for Bexley and Bromley on the London Assembly.

I’m excited to stand in these elections to highlight Green thinking that can deliver real change for Londoners to make our city more people friendly and resilient.

Every day as a mental health nurse I see the impact that the pandemic is having on people. Many are struggling to manage on low incomes and with uncertainty for the future.

I tend to see things through a public health lens and I think that Green Party policies which see people as part of their environment provide solid solutions.

We need a new way of doing things that will protect the health of Londoners by reducing pollution and providing more financial security.

I’d like to see a rapid move away from polluting cars with more inclusive space for walking and cycling that gives everyone the chance to move around under their own steam.

We know that air pollution is killing people. In places like Bromley and Bexley we have an excess of car use. There’s a real opportunity here to transform our local neighbourhoods and make them more people friendly.

The time has come for Universal Basic Income to provide a secure financial base for everyone during these times of uncertainty as we transition away from fossil fuels and towards more automation.

I’d like to see a trial of UBI in London now that the pandemic has reinforced that this is less a fringe idea and more of a necessity.

Sian and Caroline have been working really hard to deliver change. I’m hopeful that we can get more Greens on the assembly and do even more to make London the greenest city in the world.

Gemma lives in Shortlands on Beckenham Grove with her husband and two cats, since 2018.

Gemma first moved to London in 2010 when she was the first in her family to go to University. Following University Gemma has built a career in Healthcare Administration as a contractor working with diverse client bases such as NHS Trusts, Universities, Councils & Corporate Clients with their Occupational Health needs.

Gemma has a track record of campaigning on local issues that matter to residents whether that be from getting extra capacity on busy bus routes to ensuring local streets are kept clean.

Gemma said: "Bromley Council already have a proven track record of keeping Council Tax low whilst running award winning services, but I know there is more that can be done and over the coming weeks I hope to earn the trust of the residents in Shortlands and Park Langley so that I can sit with Felicity and Adam in the Civic Centre fighting for Shortlands & Park Langley. I am very much looking forward to the campaign ahead!"

In her free time, Gemma enjoys visiting historical sites, fashion, reading and mountaineering. One of Gemma’s ambitions over the coming years is to complete the Everest Base Camp trek.

I am honoured to stand as a candidate for Reform UK in the constituency I have called home for nearly three decades. Beckenham & Penge is a vibrant and diverse community, encompassing residents from all walks of life. I am deeply committed to ensuring that every voice is heard - a right that has significantly diminished over the past two decades under successive Conservative and Labour governments.

Having experienced life as a single parent, small business owner, and taxpayer, I believe my background reflects the experiences of many in our community. This enables me to more realistically articulate the interests and concerns of the constituents I wish to represent.

The decline we have all witnessed is profoundly troubling. Unsustainable mass immigration, the economic strain caused by soaring taxes and the exorbitant rise in the cost of food, housing & energy bills. Declining public services with millions languishing on NHS waiting lists, in some cases for years not to mention the almost impossibility of getting a face to face with a GP, to the absurd wokeism that has infected our institutions including our Police, schools & universities.

On top of this we have the nonsensical blind charge towards a completely foolhardy and impossible to achieve net zero target. This will only serve to impoverish the most vulnerable in society and make our nation poorer and less productive.

The challenges we face are severe and urgent.

Politicians of the two main Party’s have proven over many, many decades to be arrogant, inept, out of touch, blatantly dishonest and ultimately dismissive of the wishes of the British public to be trusted any longer. In short I believe we have all been taken for fools for far too long and it’s about time we said enough!

Reform UK is the only Party to offer common sense policies based on the needs of the people rather than on ridiculous virtue signalling ideologies that have no meaning whatsoever to the vast majority of British citizens

I’m a dedicated public service policy expert who knows what good and bad council policy looks like.

Like many, I’ve experienced challenges in life, from living as someone who is partially deaf, to facing housing insecurity during Covid after standing up to my landlord over problems with my home.

That’s why I’m standing - to help you and your family tackle unfairness in your lives.

I’ve been jogging the streets of Shortlands for years – long before I decided to stand for election. You might know me from walking Kosmo on Queensmead Rec (he's the one who never goes anywhere without his ball thrower). I'm also the Site Secretary at Shortlands Allotment – where I’ve somehow managed to grow beetroot, sweet potato and bell peppers.

I was born and raised in East Dulwich, and I’ve called Shortlands home since 2020. I’m a Chartered Management Accountant and spent four years living and working in Belgium.

For the last 10 years I’ve been working as a finance director at one of the world’s leading sustainability and climate advisory firms, so I understand how to deliver long-term value and, most importantly, make money work for residents — not just contractors.

I know this isn't where the Greens usually win. But I believe residents here want a candidate who tells the truth, brings fresh ideas, and works hard for the area — not someone expecting an easy ride.

Bromley Council is failing on the basics – from potholes to speeding on roads like Glassmill Lane. Too often services are cut or outsourced, and residents end up paying more for less. We need a council that actually invests in our area and plans properly for the future – from our parks to our high streets – not continued decline.

On planning, there are proposals to build 2,000 homes on Hayes Farm. Our current councillors won’t oppose it directly – I will. We need genuinely affordable homes, but built in the right places, not on valuable green space.

I support the Green Party because protecting our local businesses, community and environment isn’t extreme – it’s common sense.

During this election campaign I'll be running every street in this ward, listening to residents. If you see me, stop me for a chat – I’d love to meet you.

I have lived in the Park Langley and Shortlands area for most of my life and have always enjoyed a positive relationship with the local community.

In my role as a pastoral manager in a secondary school, I have seen firsthand the value of youth services in supporting young people’s wellbeing. I have a strong interest in ensuring that teenagers have access to meaningful activities and appropriate support.

I value diversity and inclusion for all residents of Bromley, regardless of race, gender, disability, or age. I am concerned about the increasing climate of hostility in England and believe in fostering a respectful and welcoming environment.

I am also committed to the protection of Bromley’s green spaces and the preservation of local wildlife. In addition, I would welcome the development of more community spaces, including public garden areas that everyone can enjoy growing fruit and vegetables.

I'm a lifelong resident in the area and new to politics. I’m standing to reverse the decline in our local services and environment. I’ve run local businesses for over 30 years.

I’m committed to making Bromley a great place for businesses to grow and for young people to live, learn and succeed. I'll work to attract more investment into the Borough which will help bring jobs and opportunities as well as boosting growth.

This election is about restoring common sense at a local level. I will be a strong local voice for you and ensure that your best interests are served at the council. I will work to protect our green spaces from unsuitable housing developments, improve the quality of our roads and support measures which cut crime and tackle anti-social behaviour.

Nathaniel has been a resident of St Mary Cray Ward with my family since 2006. He has been working as a Probation Service Practitioner since 2005 after ten years in retail management.

Halimot Ibrahim. LLB Law & LLM International Relations. Advocate for equality & mental wellbeing. St Mary Cray Ward

I have proudly called St. Mary Cray home for over three years, and my family’s roots are firmly planted here. I am a law graduate with a background in international relations, and I currently work for a charity supporting fathers to build stronger relationships with their children. I also co-founded a women’s organisation, helping women access guidance, support, and hope during difficult times.

I joined the Green Party because I believe in putting people, community, and the planet first. I care deeply about improving our standard of living while protecting the green spaces that make our area special. Through volunteering locally — including with Friends of the Park — I have seen how powerful community action can be.

I want to work alongside residents to build a safer, greener, more connected St. Mary Cray. Together, we can strengthen neighbourhood safety, protect our parks, support local businesses, and create meaningful programmes and safe spaces for young people.

This campaign is not just about policies, it’s about people. If you have ideas, energy, or a passion for our community, I would love to work with you to make them happen.

I'm Jake Rest, and I've lived here in St Mary Cray my entire life. I know this community, and I’m ready to fight for the things that matter most to you.

When I talk to residents, the same issues keep coming up. Our roads are crumbling with potholes that damage your cars. Our streets don't feel as safe as they should. We don’t need flags and symbols of division to make that point. Real safety comes from investment in a community that actually looks after one another.

As your council representative, I'll be your direct line to action on everyday issues that affect your life. I'll tackle the smaller things that make a real difference: maintaining our local infrastructure, making St Mary Cray safer for everyone and protecting our green spaces.

I've joined the Green Party because I believe we've reached a turning point. Too many of our politicians serve the interests of big corporations and the wealthy elite, while ordinary people are left behind. We need something different and politics that puts YOU first, not corporate profits or divisive nationalism.

The Greens stand for real change: a living wage that pays, investment in green jobs and transport, and genuinely affordable housing. We're building a party that unites people around hope, not division.

I'm running to bring that hope to St Mary Cray. To fix what's broken locally and fight for a fairer future for all of us. You deserve better. Let's build it together.

As a resident, it often feels like St Paul's Cray has been neglected and that's why I'm standing up.
